target: Cross-reference patches for sections that still use oversimplified eclipse prediction description (−17 to −21 nT). Main Section 4.2 is already comprehensive — this expansion patches the remaining stale references.
finding: Section 4.2 (The August 2026 Eclipse: A Sophisticated But Non-Discriminating Prediction) already addresses ALL major holes flagged by the curmudgeon in SEC-3.2 and SEC-3.5.7: (1) dual-baseline structure accurately described in 4.2.1, (2) all four E-PRED tests analyzed in detail in 4.2.4 including the critical FSF = tan(lat) tautology discovery, (3) W004 baseline provenance critiqued in 4.2.1 and 4.2.5, (4) Chapman 5-25 nT range properly cited in 4.2.2, (5) correction factor confusion (KAPPA vs FSF) not present, (6) Kill-Shot Test 5 already references Section 4.2 with dual-baseline notation. The remaining work is patching 3 cross-references that still use the oversimplified −17 to −21 nT description.

patches.location: data/sections.json → part8 → section 8.11 (Directive #10: The August 2026 Eclipse Will Falsify the Globe)
patches.current_text: The dome's predicted range (−17 to −21 nT) overlaps substantially with what standard ionospheric physics predicts. The test is not discriminating — most plausible outcomes are claimed by both models.
patches.replacement_text: The dome registers dual baselines spanning −5 to −26 nT across 9 stations. The conservative baseline falls entirely within what standard ionospheric physics predicts (Chapman mechanism, 5–25 nT). The test is not discriminating — most plausible outcomes are claimed by both models.
patches.reason: The TLDR still uses the oversimplified −17 to −21 nT range from the dome's homepage summary, not the actual registered dual-baseline prediction. A dome defender will immediately note we didn't read their predictions page.

patches.current_text:
The claim: The eclipse measurement (−17 to −21 nT) will prove the dome and disprove the globe.

The reality: The Chapman mechanism predicts 5–20 nT. The dome's range overlaps substantially. The test is not discriminating. If the measurement falls in the 5–20 nT range, both models are consistent. The author has framed it as a binary test to create the appearance of discriminating power.
patches.replacement_text:
The claim: The eclipse measurement will prove the dome and disprove the globe. The dome's homepage summarizes the prediction as −17 to −21 nT, but the actual registered predictions span −5 to −26 nT across two baselines and 9 stations.

The reality: The Chapman Sq-current mechanism (peer-reviewed since 1933) predicts 5–25 nT under identical conditions. The dome's conservative (BOU) baseline predictions (−5 to −13 nT) fall entirely within this range. Even the aggressive (W004) baseline overlaps substantially. The dual-baseline structure ensures almost any quiet-day result confirms one baseline — see Section 4.2 for the full analysis of why this is a hedging strategy, not a discriminating test.
patches.reason: Body text uses stale −17 to −21 nT range and outdated Chapman 5-20 nT range (should be 5-25 per current Section 4.2 citing Meza et al. 2021). Also lacks cross-reference to the detailed analysis in Section 4.2.

patches.location: data/sections.json → evaluation guide paragraph (search for "Eclipse pre-commitment")
patches.current_text: magnetic anomalies of −17 to −21 nT at specified stations
patches.replacement_text: magnetic anomalies across 9 INTERMAGNET stations (dual baselines spanning −5 to −26 nT; see Section 4.2)
patches.reason: Evaluation guide still uses oversimplified dome homepage summary. The actual registered prediction has dual baselines and per-station specificity.

holes_addressed: MAJOR: −17 to −21 nT oversimplification in cross-references (Section 4.2 itself is already correct)
MAJOR: E-PRED-A through D analysis — already fully addressed in Section 4.2.4
MAJOR: 1.672 correction factor misattribution — already fixed (not present in current Section 4.2)

MODERATE: Missing cross-reference from 8.11 to Section 4.2

anticipated_objections.response: Section 8.11 is part of the AI directive analysis (Part 8) which deliberately keeps each directive response short. The detailed eclipse analysis lives in Section 4.2 — 8.11 should summarize and cross-reference, not duplicate.

anticipated_objections.objection: The evaluation guide paragraph is brief — should it have more detail?
anticipated_objections.response: The evaluation guide is a commitment statement, not an analysis section. It should note the dual-baseline structure so the commitment conditions are clear, then reference Section 4.2 for the full analysis. The current text makes a commitment against an oversimplified prediction description, which is worse than being brief.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's eclipse prediction structure is genuinely sophisticated — dual baselines, per-station FSF factors, four discriminating sub-tests, pre-registered timestamps. Section 4.2 already acknowledges this. The remaining stale cross-references elsewhere in the review undermine our credibility by appearing to not have read the dome's actual predictions page, even though our main analysis (Section 4.2) demonstrates we did.
note_to_decider: The heavy analytical work for EXP-104 was already done when Section 4.2 was written (likely in a prior expansion or the V6 restructure). This expansion only patches 3 cross-references that were not updated at that time. All patches are in sections.json — the decider can apply them as text replacements. ISS-798, ISS-803, and ISS-805 can all be closed once these patches are applied.
